title: Singleton
description: Ensure a class has only one instance with global access

title: Factory Method
description: Define an interface for creating objects; let subclasses decide the concrete type

title: Abstract Factory
description: Create families of related objects without specifying concrete classes

title: Builder
description: Construct complex objects step by step using the same construction process

title: Prototype
description: Create new objects by cloning existing ones

title: Adapter
description: Allow incompatible interfaces to work together by wrapping one in a translator

title: Bridge
description: Decouple an abstraction from its implementation so both can vary independently

title: Composite
description: Compose objects into tree structures and treat individual objects and compositions uniformly

title: Decorator
description: Attach additional responsibilities to an object dynamically

title: Facade
description: Provide a simplified interface to a library, framework, or complex subsystem

title: Flyweight
description: Share common state to support large numbers of fine-grained objects efficiently

title: Proxy
description: Provide a surrogate or placeholder that controls access to another object

title: Chain of Responsibility
description: Pass requests along a chain of handlers, each deciding to process or pass on

title: Command
description: Encapsulate a request as an object to support undo, queuing, and logging

title: Interpreter
description: Define a grammar for a language and provide an interpreter to evaluate sentences

title: Iterator
description: Provide a way to sequentially access elements of a collection without exposing its representation

title: Mediator
description: Define an object that encapsulates how a set of objects interact, promoting loose coupling

title: Memento
description: Capture and restore an object's internal state without violating encapsulation

title: Observer
description: Define a subscription mechanism to notify multiple objects of state changes

title: State
description: Allow an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes

title: Strategy
description: Define a family of algorithms, encapsulate each one, and make them interchangeable

title: Template Method
description: Define the skeleton of an algorithm in a base class, deferring steps to subclasses

title: Visitor
description: Separate an algorithm from the object structure it operates on using double dispatch

title: Single Responsibility
description: A class should have only one reason to change

title: Open/Closed Principle
description: Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification

title: Liskov Substitution
description: Subtypes must be substitutable for their base types without altering correctness

title: Interface Segregation
description: Clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they don't use

title: Dependency Inversion
description: Depend on abstractions, not on concretions
